author: PC Gamer
text:
The Adventures of Elliot: The Millennium Tales review
The Adventures of Elliot finds joy in speedrunning history.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Forza Horizon 6's latest patch may have quelled Bowie Knife99's rage alongside making it easier for players to 100% regions
No more endless searching for lost roads.

author: PC Gamer
text:
The DualSense can still drift, but Sony may be exploring magnetic tech for shape-shifting buttons in whatever comes next
Worth a poke.

author: PC Gamer
text:
It just keeps getting worse—Microsoft reportedly already had Ninja Theory in its crosshairs while pushing the announcement trailer for its next game
"Microsoft had already planned to sunset or split with the studio."

author: PC Gamer
text:
'Half-Life 2 RTX has shrunk from 80 GB to 50 GB': RTX Remix 1.5 update shrinks file sizes, and also starts letting in AI agents
I suppose that's one way to bring an old game up to date…

author: PC Gamer
text:
There's no such thing as the Matt Mercer Effect
You just need a better session zero.

author: PC Gamer
text:
I ran 45 idle game demos from Steam Next Fest all at once for a desktop companion battle royale
Steam Next Fest is packed, but I've found a way to manage.

author: PC Gamer
text:
I need you to play this unbelievably tense 2 hour horror about descending into hell with a grappling hook
Idols of Ash is $3, has over 2,000 Overwhelmingly Positive reviews on Steam, and is one of my favourites so far of 2026.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Bungie will let Destiny 2 'near infinite damage' bug ride for a week: 'Do your thing'
Players are also back to punting bosses off of ledges.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Attention former 2000s youths: A second Beyblade-inspired roguelike has hit Steam Next Fest
It's a big year for battle tops.

author: PC Gamer
text:
'World of Warcraft Camelot' is a mysterious new branch of the MMO that Blizzard fans are convinced is the first real evidence of a remixed WoW Classic
September's BlizzCon might be where we finally hear about "WoW Classic Plus".

author: PC Gamer
text:
All Grow a Garden 2 codes for June 2026: Earn sheckles with free seeds
Start growing your garden with active codes for seeds and other freebies.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Less than a month before launch, survival RPG Outward 2 delayed into 2027
"I apologize that we couldn’t give you the game you guys deserve on time. "

author: PC Gamer
text:
Overwatch season 3 kicks off with the gift of Jetpack Cat nerfs after the Apache helicopter meta dominated the World Cup
Shoot the cat down.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Sega says it made a 'life-sized figure containing Sonic's DNA,' and I have a few questions
According to Sega, Sonic's DNA "essence" has been captured with "cutting-edge biotechnology."

author: PC Gamer
text:
Silicon Motion says 'the retail SSD market has almost disappeared' as NAND shifts towards AI servers and OEMs scoop up the drives that usually sit in our gaming PCs
Yet another day during the RAMpocalypse.

author: PC Gamer
text:
An obscure new game-breaking Destiny bug lets you one-hit bosses, and I don't think Bungie's gonna let this one slide (Update: Bungie is letting it slide... for a little bit)
Somehow, the Cratening has returned.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Thanks for the Soup is the most fun I've had in a horror game in a long time, and that's owing to the broccoli
Broccoli seeds + medium fertilizer = cash.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Rode NT1 5th Generation microphone review
An unclippable king of the digital and analogue realms.

author: PC Gamer
text:
I've been waiting 20 years for a worthy sequel to my favourite life sim, but THQ Nordic has other ideas
The Guild: Europa 1410 ain't it.

author: PC Gamer
text:
One lone legend waiting for GTA 6 to launch has decided to make the game from scratch themselves: 'The goal: beat the real GTA 6 to launch'
Vibe code GTA 6? Sure, hold my beer.

author: PC Gamer
text:
A new flip phone that blocks social media at a system level is coming out, and you'll never guess which retro gaming company is making it
It's billed as 'less scroll, and more soul.'

author: PC Gamer
text:
Embark is finally cracking down on Arc Raiders' cheaters and duplication antics: 'Our commitment to anti-cheat initiatives remains a top priority'
Denuvo Anti-Cheat is rolling out to the masses.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Phanteks XT View Matrix case review
Ding ding ding, we have a winner.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Mistfall Hunter's demo might finally convince me, a souls sicko, to give extraction games a proper go—even if it's more modern God of War than Nightreign
Kratos, is that you?

author: PC Gamer
text:
Arc Raiders is finally testing a ban on free loadouts, and I'm here for it
Embark is grabbing the science kit and monitoring your feedback.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Intel's first chip with Nvidia graphics is coming in early 2028 according to the latest leak and it could mark a new era for handheld PC gaming
Nvidia's second-gen RTX Spark is also due in 2028 and AMD has new chips coming, too.

author: PC Gamer
text:
SteelSeries Arctis Nova 7X Wireless Gen 2 review
A helpful refinement.

author: PC Gamer
text:
There are stirrings in the VR industry that much better displays could be in the pipeline
Samsung is apparently leaning more heavily into Micro-OLED.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Viewsonic unveils the world's first 24-inch 4K gaming monitor for super crispy visuals in a smaller form factor
160 Hz and 1 ms response, to boot.

author: PC Gamer
text:
PlayerUnknown's survival game becomes free-to-own and offers Steam refunds 'without restrictions on playtime or how long ago you bought the game'
It's free forever, but it's also over.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Hours after announcing a collab with Fortnite, Vampire Survivors dev suggests it may back out over Epic's touting of generative AI
Poncle is apparently reconsidering its Fortnite collab after seeing how Epic is using generative AI to help design Fortnite assets.

author: PC Gamer
text:
'[It] is going to change a lot about how games are made': Epic merges Unreal Engine 5 with Unreal Engine for Fortnite to give game devs around the world Unreal Engine 6
When two become one.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Guild Wars 3 hopes to solve the problem that made me quit Guild Wars 2 after thousands of hours with the series
I stopped logging in after realizing I would always need to clean my room before I was allowed to have any fun.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Capcom has heard feedback that Onimusha is too easy, says it's 'confident fans will be satisfied with difficulty' in the final game
The behind-closed-doors Onimusha demo at Summer Game Fest was fantastic, but easy.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Rockstar faces legal setback as UK tribunal allows its fired workers to bring every one of their union busting allegations to trial
The trial date for Rockstar's firings dispute is now set for September.

author: PC Gamer
text:
'It feels to many like a tidal wave is sweeping over the AAA game business,' says Epic CEO Tim Sweeney in his diagnosis of the industry's woes
As the AAA model falters, Sweeney wants developers to build a "global ecosystem" of Unreal Engine games to rival Roblox.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Unreal Engine 5.8 launches with improved terrain and vegetation tools, a Lumen Lite option for faster global illumination, and for the times we now live in, an open standard plugin for LLM systems
Plus a whole heap more stuff that's genuinely useful for all kinds of devs.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Windows 11 Search will soon see through your typos and actually find what you're looking for
Dotting the 'i's, crossing the 't's.

author: PC Gamer
text:
All roads lead to philosophy, as this Wikipedia game proves
It's all footnotes to Plato, just as Whitehead said.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Stellaris' Nomads expansion finally lets me live out my Battlestar Galactica fantasies, though its Wayline system could definitely use some work
It's also got Mongolian throat singing.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Intel's 18A-P chipmaking process has started risk production and promises 9% improved CPU performance for the same power
Or 18% less power consumption for the same performance.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Warning all weebs: Kaspersky says hackers are distributing malware via anime girl wallpapers on Steam Workshop's Wallpaper Engine
Is nothing sacred?

author: PC Gamer
text:
Don't Kill Them All make you keep orc calm so orc not smash resources—big smart, neat game, like demo much
Me can be different kind of orc.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Civilization is the most iconic turn-based series of all time, but Sid Meier considered making it an RTS: 'It could have easily gone in different directions'
The origins of Civ are also the origins of Age of Empires.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang says we need to 'deal with social norms' surrounding AI but apparently that means 'just go engage it'
Oh, right, of course that's exactly what he has in mind. Use AI, got it.

author: PC Gamer
text:
The Adventures of Elliot: The Millennium Tales review
The Adventures of Elliot finds joy in speedrunning history.
